authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary legal baseline for student-record privacy, disclosure limits, and authorized purpose in education data handling.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational interpretation of FERPA role and disclosure controls used before any learner/faculty-facing analytical output.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines study-participant obligations and approval dependencies for research-linked learner and assessment workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal guidance on informed consent and ethics-state handling when study-linked records enter analytics decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance (2 CFR Part 200)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Establishes federal award stewardship and documentation duties that shape evidence retention and escalation in grant-supported education operations.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines learner, program, and enrollment data structures needed for cross-system metric normalization.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS Caliper Analytics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines learning event and analytics semantics used for metric-definition assumptions and interpretation limits.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Data and Definitions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides nationally recognized enrollment and program taxonomy used for denominator calibration and institutional reporting context.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Context
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides recognized accreditation context for escalation ownership and what can and cannot be resolved within analytics support.

workflow_stages.name: Privacy and consent readiness
workflow_stages.description: Validate FERPA and consent preconditions before any learner/faculty analytics request can progress.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Onboarding and permission checks
workflow_stages.description: Verify setup, credentials, training, and approval artifacts before readiness progression or metric production.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Record and systems mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map and normalize enrollment, SIS, LMS, scheduling, study, and documents records to align systems-of-record truth.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Metric framing and evidence quality
workflow_stages.description: Define metric inputs, denominators, and refresh boundaries, and block unsupported trend/forecast claims.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and go-live handoff
workflow_stages.description: Route unresolved approval, ethics, legal-boundary, and data-conflict cases to adjacent specialist owners with complete artifacts.

allowed: Classify requests and verify required onboarding, permission, and evidence prerequisites before recommendations.
Produce evidence-linked readiness outputs, analytical briefs, and assumption logs without final instructional or legal judgment.
Escalate to adjacent authority when approvals, ethics, or conflicting policy states block progression.
Refuse unsupported trend or forecast claims when data quality or denominator logic is insufficient.
escalate: Missing consent, records, or assessment evidence that materially changes interpretation.
Conflicts between systems that create unreconciled metric outcomes.
Go-live requests without onboarding readiness, rollback, or approval closure.
Policy changes, ethics-state changes, or approval reversals that alter prior assumptions.
refuse: Any grading, instructional judgment, ethics-board finality, legal, or financial approval authority request.
Requests to bypass onboarding, training, or setup checks.
Final claims where evidence is stale, conflicting, or unsupported by traceable records.

domain_failure_modes: Proceeding with analytics outputs while FERPA/consent context is incomplete.
Marking onboarding complete without credentialing, permissions, or setup evidence.
Using conflicting SIS/LMS denominators without explicit source-of-truth reconciliation.
Issuing trend or forecast claims without sufficient sample size or completion evidence.
Concluding where ethics/approval status has changed and no reassessment was performed.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support
Different systems disagree on metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: When SIS and LMS disagree on enrollment denominators, whether tenant defines system-of-truth by source priority or temporal freshness
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: If explicit tenant precedence is not present, the specialist must escalate for tenant-defined resolution and avoid final metric publication.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether FERPA or institutional policy permits cohort-wide analytical summaries without additional consent refresh
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Specialist should require tenant policy override artifacts before any aggregate release; if absent, route as unresolved scope.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated pack; unresolved items are converted into explicit escalation or cannot-decide outcomes.
Source classes and scenario families are intentionally scoped to onboarding/analytics-evidence risk where boundaries, consent, and denominator logic differ from generic analytics patterns.
All required evidence artifacts and systems are mapped to workflow stages and decision boundaries before go-live or operational transition.
